summary: Set 069 Session 3 of 6: the probe-template lane -- the proposal's 'missing middle' (rung 4) between trigger-only command execution (S2) and the Podman model-authored-probe lane (S4). Ran on claude-opus-4-8 medium (operator launch; the routed rec). The probe-template lane lets the automated pull-critique critic find NOVEL-BUT-LOCAL edge cases by invoking OPERATOR-AUTHORED, VERSIONED probe harnesses with TYPED, validated args -- the harness stays in the trusted-command model (runs in the same disposable-worktree cage as run_test); the model supplies ONLY typed inputs, never code, never argv. ALL ADDITIVE -- absent a ProbeTemplateConfig the loop/producer are byte-for-byte the Set 067/068/S2 behavior. SHIPPED CODE. NEW MODULE ai_router/probe_templates.py: (1) declarations -- ArgSpec (string/int/bool/enum) + ProbeTemplate (versioned; names the REAL PUBLIC entrypoint it drives, entrypoint_kind in PUBLIC_ENTRYPOINT_KINDS so the meta-oracle holds by construction); (2) validate_template_args -- required-present / exact-type (int rejects bool, L-066-1) / enum-membership / no-unknown-keys / None->{} / NEVER raises; (3) the in-cage driver harness (_PROBES + _driver_main, run as `python -m ai_router.probe_templates --run <id> <json-args>`): because the cage cwd is the disposable checkout, the driver imports the code UNDER REVIEW from that worktree and drives its public entrypoint, printing ONE DETERMINISTIC PROBE_RESULT line (exception TYPE names / result codes only -- no addresses/temp paths/messages) so a pristine replay reproduces the same outputHash; exit 1=reproduced / 0=robust / 2=probe-error; (4) ProbeTemplateConfig + run_probe_template (cage-backed via run_test_in_cage; captures a ProbeRun ONLY for a clean run AND exit_code==PROBE_REPRODUCED_EXIT -- a robust/error run cannot back a reproduction; exit 2 surfaced as is_error); (5) build_probe_argv (deterministic, sort_keys); (6) the SEED LIBRARY BUILTIN_PROBE_TEMPLATES (malformed_artifact_bytes -> validate_path_aware_critique_artifact on malformed bytes; bad_parent_dir -> run_test_in_cage with a bad worktrees_parent) -- the two 0.22.x bug classes. ai_router/pull_verifier.py: RUN_PROBE_TEMPLATE_TOOL + _run_probe_template_tool_schema (lists the library + typed args) + _dispatch_run_probe_template (cage dispatch OUTSIDE the byte-equality guard, like run_test); _Execution gains kind/template_id/template_args/entrypoint_kind/entrypoint_ref AND its OWN replay context repo_root/ref/caps (the R2 fix); _verdict_tool_schema(allow_template_evidence) adds a templateId proposal field gated on the template lane; _stamp_evidence_tiers is LANE-KEYED (commandId resolves only against command executions, templateId only against template ones; both-ids -> collapse) and is now CFG-FREE (replay/transcript use each execution's own captured repo/ref/caps so a mixed-lane run never replays against the wrong tree); _build_transcript emits a templateId+args transcript with the template's PUBLIC entrypoint for a template execution; pull_route offers run_probe_template when configured + dispatches + stamps. ai_router/pull_critique.py: produce_path_aware_critique threads probe_template_config to pull_route (+ it counts as an execution lane for blast-radius-budgeted caps); --probe-templates CLI flag (requires --exec-ref) builds a ProbeTemplateConfig with the builtin library. BUG FOUND + FIXED BY THE DOGFOOD (ai_router/path_aware_critique.py): the malformed_artifact_bytes class surfaced a STILL-LATENT instance of the 0.22.x UnicodeError class -- four readers (read_path_aware_critique, has_path_aware_critique_record, path_aware_critique_record_unreadable, validate_path_aware_critique_artifact's file-read path) caught only (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) and would crash close-out on invalid-UTF-8 bytes (a UnicodeError, NOT a JSONDecodeError -- the same class Set 068 fixed in contract_gate but left in this sibling). Added UnicodeError to all four + a regression class TestMalformedBytesNeverRaise. SCOPE-NOTED RESIDUAL: ~20 more (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) sites exist across ai_router; the broader sweep is deferred (a future set), and only the path_aware_critique readers (on THIS set's close-out path + the seed template's target) are fixed here -- recorded as a decision, not an oversight (new lesson L-069-1: a bug is a bug CLASS -- grep all siblings or scope+record the residual). DOC: ai_router/docs/pull-verifier.md gains a 'What Set 069 S3 added' section. VERIFICATION (cross-provider, gpt-5-4, gated->REQUIRED -- the 5-file ai_router+docs diff trips routed_gate on breadth; up-front conventions block per L-064-10). R1 FAIL 3 findings: (1) a clean template run was captured regardless of exit code -> a robust(0)/error(2) run could back REPRODUCED -> gated capture on exit==1; (2) commandId/templateId shared one untyped match map -> cross-lane mis-bind -> lane-keyed maps + both-ids collapse; (3) missing negative tests -> added. R2 confirmed all 3 AND raised 1 new: a residual shared-replay-cfg defect when both lanes have different configs -> FIXED by storing repo/ref/caps on each _Execution and making replay/transcript/stamp cfg-free + a proving test. R3 PASS. Full ai_router suite GREEN: 1820 passed / 1 pre-existing skip (baseline 1767 + 53 new across the four test files). Metered verification ~$0.705 (R1 $0.317 + R2 $0.258 + R3 $0.130) + next-session rec $0.004. NO release this session (PyPI is S6); ai_router version unchanged; NO Marketplace/extension change (spec non-goal: no UI this whole set). DEFERRED to later sessions: the Podman model-authored-probe lane gated on a green spike (S4), the ceiling->floor ratchet + measured replacement gate (S5), the release + dogfood (S6), and the broader ai_router UnicodeError sweep (a future set). Next-session rec routed (next-session-rec.md): anthropic/sonnet-4-6/medium for S4's spike-gated Podman systems coding (the analysis model returned a stale model name, mapped to the closest current candidate; switch from opus for cost given the well-defined, spike-gated nature).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (next-session-rec.md) recommends anthropic/sonnet-4-6 medium for S4's spike-gated Podman lane: a well-defined, pre-validated systems-implementation task where strong instruction-following over raw generation makes Sonnet the cheapest-capable choice (it returned a stale model name, mapped to the closest current candidate). S4 FIRST reads podman-spike/spike-result.json: if NOT GREEN/absent, record NO-GO and skip the Podman work (proceed to S5 with rungs 1-3+5-6). Operator may instead pick codex gpt-5.4/high or claude opus-4-8/high; cross-provider verification stays REQUIRED (the diff will trip routed_gate).
